rationale: Both correctly latch onto the thread's one confirmed fact (yenuo26 blaming PR #4834) and build a plausible, well-cited narrative around the NotImplementedError guard and a level=1 fix, but the actual thread never confirms any root cause or fix — it ends with Flink-ddd still asking for a full CI run, i.e. genuinely unresolved. Both candidates overreach by presenting a full diagnosis and 'close' disposition as fact rather than hedging. Y overreaches further, explicitly claiming the fix is 'already applied on main' and citing an existing passing regression test as proof — a specific, falsifiable claim directly contradicted by the thread's open, still-investigating state. X frames its fix as a proposal rather than asserting it already landed, making it slightly better calibrated to the actual (inconclusive) thread.
